title: rosa communis var. litigiosa
intro: Rosa communis var. litigiosa, also known as wild rose or dog rose, is a perennial shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and fever. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. litigiosa include its ability to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
